summary: Linked src/melee/lb/lb_00CE.c by marking it Matching in configure.py, adjusting lb_00CE .sdata2 symbol metadata from global lb_804D7Axx names to local @ labels, adding a small sdata2_order helper to force float/double literal ordering, and simplifying lb_8000D148 distance-squared expressions. The bot report credited 2 new matches: lb_00CE .sdata2 to 100% and lb_8000D148 to 100%.

key_files.role: Primary source change: include adjustments, added sdata2_order helper referencing constants in order, and rewrote lb_8000D148 squared-distance locals into direct expressions.
Changed lb_00CE object status from NonMatching to Matching.
Updated .sdata2 symbols at 0x804D7A68 through 0x804D7AA4 from global lb_804D7Axx names to local @ labels with existing size/type/alignment metadata.

what_changed.text: configure.py now treats melee/lb/lb_00CE.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.
Ten .sdata2 entries for lb_00CE-area constants were renamed/rescoped from global lb_804D7A68-style names to local @15, @16, @17, @18, @20, @22, @34, @35, @36, and @37 labels.
src/melee/lb/lb_00CE.c added placeholder.h and switched the math_ppc include spelling to <math_ppc.h> while retaining platform, baselib, math, and trigf includes.
A static sdata2_order function was added that void-references M_PI_2, M_PI, 1.0f, 2.0f, S32_TO_F32, 0.0f, 0.5, 3.0, 0.00001f, and -0.00001f.
evidence_refs: review:ribbanya DISMISSED body='This is the simplest way to force float order.'

what_changed.text: lb_8000D148 removed four temporary diff_* locals and computes dist_squared_02 and dist_squared_12 directly from repeated coordinate-difference products.
The automated report recorded +420 matched-code bytes, +1180 linked-code bytes, +64 matched-data bytes, +64 linked-data bytes, with new matches for main/melee/lb/lb_00CE .sdata2 and lb_8000D148.

smart_moves.text: The PR linked the whole lb_00CE object only after pairing the configure.py status change with source and symbol metadata changes and an automated report showing both .sdata2 and lb_8000D148 reached 100%.
The distance-squared expression cleanup reduces intermediate locals while preserving the same coordinate-difference computations in the changed hunk.
Reviewer text explicitly identifies the added sdata2_order helper as a way to force float order, making the intent of that otherwise artificial-looking helper review-visible in this PR slice.
evidence_refs: review:ribbanya DISMISSED body='This is the simplest way to force float order.'

decomp_lessons.lesson: For a linking PR, object status changes in configure.py should be supported by narrow evidence that the affected unit or symbols now match; here the bot report names lb_00CE .sdata2 and lb_8000D148 reaching 100%.

When a TU's local constants are the matching issue, symbol metadata may need to represent them as local @ labels rather than address-style global names; in this PR, the .sdata2 hunk converted ten lb_804D7Axx globals to local @ labels and the report credited the unit's .sdata2 as 100%.

naming_conventions.text: This PR used local @NN labels for file-local .sdata2 constants instead of global address-style lb_804D7Axx names for the same addresses.

Function and argument names in the touched source remain descriptive for the geometry calculation, including point0_x, point0_y, point1_x, point1_y, point2_x, point2_y, threshold, threshold_squared, dist_squared_02, and dist_squared_12.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Added a static sdata2_order helper that references float and double constants in a specific order to influence .sdata2 ordering.
evidence_refs: review:ribbanya DISMISSED body='This is the simplest way to force float order.'
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR slice supports that this tactic was used and reviewer-described, but it does not include a detailed rationale for each literal or a broader standard approving this pattern.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Changed .sdata2 symbol scope/name metadata from global address-style symbols to local @ labels, aligning the symbol table with local constants in the unit.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The bot report verifies .sdata2 progress for this PR, but the slice does not include independent map/split analysis beyond the diff and report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Recomputed squared distances with direct repeated coordinate-difference expressions rather than mutating separate diff_* locals.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The report shows lb_8000D148 reached 100%, but the slice does not show the exact before/after assembly.

notes: Strong evidence for changed files, exact diff content, PR identity, merge state, and automated progress report.
PR body is empty, so author rationale is inferred only from diff plus review/bot text.
No inline review comments are loaded; one changes-requested review only says 'One sec.' and provides no actionable technical feedback.
The bot report provides match/link percentages and named new matches, but the slice does not include raw build logs, objdiff output, or assembly listings.
The sdata2_order helper is review-described as forcing float order, but this intake should not promote that artificial helper pattern as a general accepted standard without curator review.

accepted_candidate_records.title: lb_00CE linked by combining Matching status, .sdata2 local labels, and narrow source expression cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2650 linked melee/lb/lb_00CE.c by changing configure.py from NonMatching to Matching, converting ten .sdata2 address-style globals to local @ labels, adding an sdata2_order helper for float ordering, and simplifying lb_8000D148 squared-distance expressions. The decomp-dev report credited lb_00CE .sdata2 and lb_8000D148 as new 100% matches.

source_update_candidates.title: lb_00CE.c was linked as Matching in PR 2650
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2650 changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/lb/lb_00CE.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/lb/lb_00CE.c"). The bot report listed new matches for main/melee/lb/lb_00CE .sdata2 and lb_8000D148.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-level historical fact for the curator to verify against current source state.

source_update_candidates.title: Review artificial sdata2_order helpers as a narrow float-order tactic
source_update_candidates.text: This PR used a static sdata2_order helper containing void references to literals/constants, and reviewer text says: 'This is the simplest way to force float order.' If this pattern recurs, curator may want a narrowly scoped standard describing when such helpers are acceptable, what evidence is required, and how to avoid fake data/order churn.
source_update_candidates.reason: The tactic is explicit in this PR but should be reconciled with existing standards on data ownership, fake storage, and matching tactics before becoming a general rule.

source_update_candidates.title: lb_00CE .sdata2 constants changed from global lb_804D7Axx symbols to local @ labels in PR 2650
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2650 changed ten .sdata2 entries at 0x804D7A68 through 0x804D7AA4 from global lb_804D7Axx names to local @NN labels while preserving object type, size, alignment, and float/double data annotations.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential symbol metadata history useful for future data-section investigations.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that sdata2_order helpers are broadly accepted decomp style from this PR alone; the evidence shows one reviewer-approved use in one PR, not a global policy.
Do not claim the exact original-source form of lb_8000D148 beyond the diff and bot match report; no assembly or objdiff listing is included in the loaded slice.
Do not infer detailed reviewer intent from the 'One sec.' changes-requested review; no inline comments or technical explanation are present.
